zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Clark於1474年出生在英國林可爾史爾維爾布時，他的父親Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer54歲，母親Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham49歲。他於1500年與Alys在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾結婚。他去世於1576年6月17日在他的家鄉享年102歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Clark于1474年出生在英国林可爾史爾維爾布时，他的父亲Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer54岁，母亲Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham49岁。他于1500年与Alys在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾结婚。他去世于1576年6月17日在他的家乡享年102岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. variants.name: Rickert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Clark was born in 1474, in Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer, was 54 and his mother, Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham, was 49. He married Alys in 1500, in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 17 June 1576, in his hometown, at the age of 102.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Clark est né en 1474, à Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham, avait 49 ans. Il a épousé Alys en 1500, à Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 17 juin 1576, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 102 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Clark 1474 in Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer 54 und seine Mutter Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham 49. Er hat 1500 in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom Alys geheiratet. Er starb am 17. Juni 1576 im Alter von 102 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Clark è nato il nel 1474 nel Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer, aveva 54 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham, aveva 49 anni. Lui sposato Alys nel 1500, nel Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 17 giugno 1576, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 102 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Clarkが1474年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウエルボウルンで生まれた時，父Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequerは54歳，母Lady Alice Elizabeth Tateshamは49歳であった。1500年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアコウルティーンハルでAlysと結婚した。彼は1576年6月17日に故郷で102歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Clark(이)가 1474년 영국 링코실르 웰보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham(은)는 49세였다. 그는 1500년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀에서 Alys(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1576년 6월 17일 그의 고향에서 102세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Clark nasceu em 1474, em Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham, tinha 49 anos. Ele casou-se com Alys em 1500, em Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 17 de junho de 1576, em sua cidade natal, com 102 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Clark родился в 1474 г. в местности велбурн, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham, 49. Он женился на женщине по имени Alys в 1500 г. в местности коуртинолл, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 17 июня 1576 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 102 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Clark nació en 1474, en Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Clark II, Baron of the Exchequer, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Lady Alice Elizabeth Tatesham, tenía 49 años. Se casó con Alys en 1500, en Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 17 de junio de 1576, en su pueblo, a la edad de 102 años.
